description: INS-728 (match branch): prefer a predefined credential over generic HTTP auth. The user forces an HTTP Request call to the Stripe API — a service with a dedicated n8n credential (stripeApi resolves from api.stripe.com). Whether via the authentication builderHint on the first build or the PREFER_PREDEFINED_CREDENTIAL remediation warning, the node should end up on authentication 'predefinedCredentialType' with nodeCredentialType 'stripeApi', not a generic credential. Build-only: graded on the HTTP node's credential configuration, not execution. Should FAIL without the PR (the node stays on genericCredentialType).

conversation.text: Every morning at 8am, use an HTTP Request node (not the built-in Stripe node) to call the Stripe API at https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges and fetch yesterday's charges, then post the total amount to Slack #finance.

outcomeExpectations: The workflow fetches from the Stripe API using an HTTP Request node whose URL targets api.stripe.com.

That HTTP Request node authenticates with the dedicated Stripe credential: authentication is 'predefinedCredentialType' with nodeCredentialType 'stripeApi'. It must NOT use a generic credential (authentication 'genericCredentialType'). An unset or placeholder credential value is acceptable — only the credential-type selection is being judged.
